summary: Iteration 45 - Verified fix for CRITICAL redact tool bug from iteration 44. The lastSelectedRef fix successfully preserves element selection across blur events. All tested features working correctly.

issues: React CSS warning: 'Updating padding paddingBottom/Left/Right/Top' - caused by mixing shorthand and non-shorthand padding properties. Non-critical but should be cleaned up.

critical_code_review_comments: VERIFIED FIX: Editor.js lines 59-65 - lastSelectedRef correctly stores selectedElement
VERIFIED FIX: Editor.js lines 627-645 - applyRedaction uses 'selectedElement || lastSelectedRef.current' fallback
VERIFIED FIX: Editor.js lines 606-623 - applyHighlight also uses lastSelectedRef fallback
MINOR: CSS padding shorthand/non-shorthand conflict in EditableText component - consider using only individual padding properties
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 45 confirmed the redact tool fix works correctly. The lastSelectedRef useRef solution successfully preserves element selection across blur events. Complete redact flow tested: select text -> click redact -> black bar appears -> click black bar -> 'Siyah Bandı Kaldır' button appears -> click to unredact -> text restored. Margins and Indent tools also working correctly.

bug_verification.original_issue: Redact tool failed because selectedElement was cleared on blur before applyRedaction ran
bug_verification.fix_applied: Added lastSelectedRef (useRef) that stores the last non-null selectedElement, applyRedaction/applyHighlight use fallback
bug_verification.verification_result: FIXED - Complete redact flow working: select text -> click redact -> black bar -> click black bar -> remove button -> unredact
